+I am a sixth PhD candidate at the [Paul G. Allen School of Computer Science & Engineering](https://www.cs.washington.edu/) at The University of Washington, where I am advised by [Tim Althoff](http://www.timalthoff.com/). I am affiliated with [UW NLP](https://twitter.com/uwnlp).
 
-**I am looking for a full time job!** If you think we would be a good match, please reach out. Previously, I was a Student Researcher at [Google Research](https://research.google/), an ML Research Intern at [Apple Health AI](https://machinelearning.apple.com/work-with-us) and a data scientist and the second full-time employee at [HealthRhythms](https://www.healthrhythms.com).

content.text:
Found and merged them. Your "Move to Stanford" changes were a commit (`c499730`) you made while in a **detached HEAD** state (you'd checked out `HEAD~1`), so switching back to `master` left them unreferenced by any branch — invisible but safe in the reflog.

What I did:
- Located the lost commit `c499730 "Move to Stanford"` via `git reflog`; it branched off `c4e38a1`, the same point `master`'s `d7d3e4b "off the job market woo"` came from.
- `git merge c499730` into `master`. It brought in all of `_layouts/default.html` cleanly, but `_includes/about.md` conflicted because both commits rewrote the same two lines.
- Resolved the conflict in favor of the Stanford version (`I am a Postdoctoral Researcher at Stanford CS.`), which supersedes the old UW/PhD wording — both sides had already dropped the "looking for a job" sentence.
- Committed the merge → `master` is now at `9bb541c`, working tree clean.

Result: `master` contains both changes; `default.html` (32 lines) and the new Stanford bio are in place. Remaining note: this is a local merge only — run `git push` when you're ready to publish, and nothing was force-updated so it's easily reversible if you'd prefer a different conflict resolution.

- Resolved the conflict in favor of the Stanford version (`I am a Postdoctoral Researcher at Stanford CS.`), which supersedes the old UW/PhD wording — both sides had already dropped the "looking for a job" sentence.
